title: Call Transfer Settings
lede: Call Transfer is a tenant-wide setting that lets operators move a live web-dialer call to a teammate or an outbound number. It is off by default. This guide covers enabling it and choosing who can transfer.

steps.title: Open the Call Transfer tab
steps.content:
1. Go to Call Center → Contact Center.
  2. Click the Call Transfer tab (available to the main account or a Contact Center admin).

images.caption: Call Transfer: enable transfers and choose who can use them.
Call Transfer: enable transfers and choose who can use them.

steps.title: Allow operators to transfer live calls
steps.content: Turn on Allow operators to transfer live calls. This lets users move a call from the web dialer to a teammate or an outbound number (including in the Human Dialer). It is off by default.

steps.title: Choose who can transfer
steps.content:
Under Who can transfer, pick one:

  • All sub-accounts.
  • Only specific sub-accounts — then select the users allowed to transfer.

steps.title: Optionally record internal calls
steps.content: Teammate-to-teammate calls ("Call a teammate") are always logged with their outcome. In the Internal teammate calls box you can also switch on Record internal calls and Transcribe internal calls (multi-party) to capture them with a recorder agent that joins when the teammate answers. Click Save.

metaDescription: Enable tenant-wide call transfer in the iMash Contact Center: let operators transfer live calls to teammates or outbound numbers, scope who can, and record internal calls.
metaKeywords: call transfer, operator transfer, teammate, outbound, internal call recording, contact center, web dialer
